attempts.strategy: Prove projectiveLineBar_isProper axiom-clean (deeper than analogist predicted — bijectivity of algebraMap k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0) requires explicit injective+surjective chain).
attempts.code_tried: change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜 ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))); haveI tower := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl; haveI ft := Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _; have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0)) := ⟨inj via C_injective + congrArg Subtype.val, surj witness MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v via homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m:=0) + homogeneousComponent_zero⟩; haveI : IsIso (Spec.map _) := (isIso_SpecMap_iff).mpr hbij; infer_instance.
attempts.insight: The analogist's `gm-scaling-p1` report listed `IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom` as "FREE from Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…" but skipped the algebra-map step: once ProjectiveLineBar = Proj 𝒜 has its OverClass via the `algebraMap k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (because Mathlib's `instIsProperToSpecZero` lands on `Proj.toSpecZero`, not the composition), the prover had to (i) install the IsScalarTower instance, (ii) prove `algebraMap k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` bijective via the homogeneous-component-of-degree-0 ⟺ constants identification, (iii) lift to IsIso of `Spec.map`. Net: a real ~25-LOC proof closure, NOT an inferInstance one-liner. Reusable pattern: when a property is "FREE" through `Proj.toSpecZero` but the project's encoding uses an explicit `↘ Spec k̄` via algebraMap composition, the surjection-of-degree-0 step is the substantive piece.

attempts.strategy: Close the remaining 5 axiom-clean instances on Ga/Gm via Mathlib inferInstance bridges.

attempts.goal_after: all axiom-clean; `lake env lean Genus0BaseObjects.lean` → exit 0
attempts.insight: `Ga` reducedness goes via the AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine iso to Spec MvPolynomial-of-Fin-1, transported by isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion. `Gm` is more direct: IsReduced (Spec _) and IsAffine (Spec _) fire on the Localization.Away-of-domain. The LocallyOfFinitePresentation bridge for Gm needs HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff to descend to RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap, then RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap.mpr inferInstance on the algebra-finite-presentation instance. None of these were the analogist's predicted one-liner shape — each required a 1-2 step bridge but all closed axiom-clean.
